subject: fix(export): wrap-pass follow-up — callback safety, sunset signal, regression doc
body:
Addresses every wrap-pass finding (13 items) including the items the wrap
itself flagged as deferred or out-of-scope.

Callback safety (#2):
- New safeWarn() helper wraps every onWarning?.(...) call in try/catch.
  Without it, a buggy caller-supplied callback throwing during partial-weight
  failure handling would cascade into the per-font .catch (which calls
  onWarning again), producing an unhandled rejection that aborts the entire
  export. Two tests cover the path: throwing onWarning during per-font
  failure and during partial-weight failure — both must NOT break the
  export and surviving weights must still inline.

Test coverage (#3):
- New pwaCleanup.test.js (3 tests): happy path calls delete for both
  pre-rename names; missing globalThis.caches is a no-op (non-PWA env);
  rejected delete is swallowed (idempotent on missing cache).

Test fixture cleanup (#8, #9):
- Replaced the leaky `_text` field on the Blob mock with a
  WeakMap<Blob, string> lookup. Mock now uses real `new Blob([text])`;
  source recovered by reference, not by faking a field that doesn't exist
  on real Blobs.
- Removed the dead `_resetFontEmbedCache` export from fontEmbed.js.
  jest.resetModules() in beforeEach already gives a fresh module instance
  with empty caches; the explicit reset call was redundant. Per CLAUDE.md
  "Don't add features beyond what the task requires."

Assumption documentation (#5, #6):
- Code comment in ExportButtons near activeFontIds derivation flags the
  title+body assumption: if a future feature adds per-element font
  selection, this derivation must collect those font IDs too — otherwise
  exports silently fall back to system fonts.
- Code comment in fontEmbed flags the Google Fonts ACAO assumption:
  if they ever stop returning Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, every fetch
  rejects with CORS error and the warn-and-degrade path produces system-
  font output. Mitigation: the SW runtime cache buffers existing entries.

Bundle tripwire (#10):
- New src/__tests__/bundleSize.test.js (4 tests). Reads dist/assets/,
  asserts: each vendor-* chunk exists, app `index-*.js` stays under 500KB
  (well above current 349KB but well below pre-split 1099KB), pdf-lib
  stays in vendor-pdf (largest dep — must stay isolated from the app
  chunk). If a future dep is added without updating manualChunks in
  vite.config.js, this test fails before the regression ships.

Sunset verification (#13):
- DebugPill PWA Diagnostics tab now shows an `SW Caches` row enumerating
  caches.keys() and flagging stale pre-rename names with status `warn`.
  Concrete signal: when no debug report shows that warning for ~30 days
  (~Oct 2026), pwaCleanup.js is safe to remove.
- TODO.md sunset entry rewritten with that mechanism + step-by-step
  removal sequence (5 steps).

Regression scenario (#4):
- TESTING_GUIDE.md new test E8 — exact steps to verify the exported PNG/
  JPG/PDF renders with the chosen Google Fonts (not system fallback).
  Includes DebugPill log + cache row checks. Specifies the diagnostic
  drill-down if step 7 (visual font check) ever fails.

In-environment verification (#1):
- Started dev server, fetched index.html (crossorigin attribute
  confirmed), fontEmbed.js + exportHelpers.js + ExportButtons.jsx via
  Vite's module graph (full wiring confirmed). Production build's
  minified `index-*.js` confirmed to contain `font-embed-warning`
  (Is bridge), the getEmbeddedFontCSS({onWarning:...}) call (Ds), and
  `fontEmbedCSS:o` in the toCanvas options. Browser-visual verification
  (open exported file, check rendered font) is the user's step,
  documented as TESTING_GUIDE.md E8.

Test count: 153 → 164 (added: 2 onWarning-throw, 3 pwaCleanup, 4 bundle,
plus 2 fontEmbed restructured for callback-safety coverage minus 0).

Tags: export, fonts, callback-safety, pwa, debug-pill, tests, bundle, claude-md, docs
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: mobile-export-fidelity
Semver: patch
